```javascript
const supabaseKey = 'eyJhbGciOiJIUzI1NiIsInR5cCI6IkpXVCJ9.eyJpc3MiOiJzdXBhYmFzZSIsInJlZiI6Ind1Y3dweWl0enFqdWtjcGhjemhyIiwicm9sZSI6ImFub24iLCJpYXQiOjE3NDkzMjQyOTYsImV4cCI6MjA2NDkwMDI5Nn0.ci_NkSeC7Klk34egMhLw4HnQ5x08w3PHofDUMtu2DwY';

module.exports = async function (context, req) {
    const corsHeaders = {
        'Access-Control-Allow-Origin': '*',
        'Access-Control-Allow-Methods': 'GET, POST, OPTIONS',
        'Access-Control-Allow-Headers': 'Content-Type, Authorization, apikey'
    };

    if (req.method === 'OPTIONS') {
        context.res = { status: 200, headers: corsHeaders };
        return;
    }

    try {
        context.log('📱 Mobile API: Generate token request');
        
        // Handle React Native axios form-data format
        let phone_number, fcm_token;
        
        context.log('📱 Request details:', {
            method: req.method,
            contentType: req.headers['content-type'],
            hasBody: !!req.body,
            bodyType: typeof req.body,
            query: req.query
        });
        
        // Try multiple ways to get the data
        if (req.body) {
            if (typeof req.body === 'string') {
                try {
                    const parsed = JSON.parse(req.body);
                    phone_number = parsed.phone_number || parsed.phone;
                    fcm_token = parsed.fcm_token;
                } catch (e) {
                    // Parse form data manually
                    const phoneMatch = req.body.match(/name="phone_number"[^]*?([0-9]+)/);
                    const fcmMatch = req.body.match(/name="fcm_token"[^]*?([^"]+)/);
                    if (phoneMatch) phone_number = phoneMatch[1];
                    if (fcmMatch) fcm_token = fcmMatch[1];
                }
            } else if (typeof req.body === 'object') {
                phone_number = req.body.phone_number || req.body.phone;
                fcm_token = req.body.fcm_token;
            }
        }
        
        // Fallback to query parameters
        if (!phone_number) {
            phone_number = req.query?.phone_number || req.query?.phone;
        }
        if (!fcm_token) {
            fcm_token = req.query?.fcm_token;
        }
        
        context.log(`📱 Generate token for phone: ${phone_number}`);
        
        if (!phone_number) {
            return context.res = {
                status: 200,
                headers: corsHeaders,
                body: JSON.stringify({
                    status: 400,
                    message: 'Phone number is required'
                })
            };
        }
        
        // Clean phone number
        const cleanPhone = phone_number.replace(/\D/g, '');
        const phoneLastTen = cleanPhone.slice(-10);
        
        // Search for user with multiple phone formats using REST API
        const phoneFormats = [
            phoneLastTen,
            `91${phoneLastTen}`,
            `+91${phoneLastTen}`,
            cleanPhone
        ];
        
        let user = null;
        
        // Try each phone format
        for (const phoneFormat of phoneFormats) {
            const response = await fetch(`${supabaseUrl}/rest/v1/custom_users?phone=eq.${encodeURIComponent(phoneFormat)}&select=*`, {
                method: 'GET',
                headers: {
                    'Content-Type': 'application/json',
                    'Authorization': `Bearer ${supabaseKey}`,
                    'apikey': supabaseKey
                }
            });
            
            if (response.ok) {
                const data = await response.json();
                if (data && data.length > 0) {
                    user = data[0];
                    break;
                }
            }
        }
        
        if (!user) {
            context.log(`❌ User not found for phone: ${phone_number}`);
            return context.res = {
                status: 200,
                headers: corsHeaders,
                body: JSON.stringify({
                    status: 404,
                    message: 'Please sign up first, then try again.'
                })
            };
        }
        
        context.log(`✅ User found: ${user.id}`);
        
        // Generate or get existing token
        let token = user.signup_token;
        
        if (!token) {
            token = generateRandomToken(20);
            
            // Update user with new token using REST API
            const updateData = {
                signup_token: token,
                last_login: new Date().toISOString()
            };
            
            if (fcm_token) {
                updateData.fcm_token = fcm_token;
            }
            
            const updateResponse = await fetch(`${supabaseUrl}/rest/v1/custom_users?id=eq.${user.id}`, {
                method: 'PATCH',
                headers: {
                    'Content-Type': 'application/json',
                    'Authorization': `Bearer ${supabaseKey}`,
                    'apikey': supabaseKey,
                    'Prefer': 'return=minimal'
                },
                body: JSON.stringify(updateData)
            });
                
            if (!updateResponse.ok) {
                context.log.error('Failed to save token:', updateResponse.statusText);
                return context.res = {
                    status: 200,
                    headers: corsHeaders,
                    body: JSON.stringify({
                        status: 500,
                        message: 'Failed to save the signup token',
                        posted_data: phone_number
                    })
                };
            }
            
            context.log(`✅ New token generated for user: ${user.id}`);
            
            context.res = {
                status: 200,
                headers: corsHeaders,
                body: JSON.stringify({
                    status: 200,
                    message: 'Token generated successfully.',
                    data: token
                })
            };
        } else {
            // Update FCM token if provided
            if (fcm_token) {
                await fetch(`${supabaseUrl}/rest/v1/custom_users?id=eq.${user.id}`, {
                    method: 'PATCH',
                    headers: {
                        'Content-Type': 'application/json',
                        'Authorization': `Bearer ${supabaseKey}`,
                        'apikey': supabaseKey,
                        'Prefer': 'return=minimal'
                    },
                    body: JSON.stringify({
                        fcm_token: fcm_token,
                        last_login: new Date().toISOString()
                    })
                });
            }
            
            context.log(`✅ Existing token returned for user: ${user.id}`);
            
            context.res = {
                status: 200,
                headers: corsHeaders,
                body: JSON.stringify({
                    status: 200,
                    message: 'Token already exists.',
                    token: token
                })
            };
        }
        
    } catch (error) {
        context.log.error('❌ Error in generate-token:', error);
        
        context.res = {
            status: 500,
            headers: corsHeaders,
            body: JSON.stringify({
                status: 500,
                message: 'Internal server error'
            })
        };
    }
};

// Helper function to generate random token
function generateRandomToken(length) {
    const chars = 'abcdefghijklmnopqrstuvwxyzABCDEFGHIJKLMNOPQRSTUVWXYZ0123456789';
    let result = '';
    for (let i = 0; i < length; i++) {
        result += chars.charAt(Math.floor(Math.random() * chars.length));
    }
    return result;
}
```

I see the issue is still there. Looking at your logs more carefully, I notice the mobile app is sending `"data": {"_parts": [Array]}` which means React Native is creating a FormData object. Let me create a more comprehensive solution that handles the FormData parsing correctly:

```javascript
// Mobile-compatible Azure Function for check-phone-exists
const supabaseUrl = 'https://wucwpyitzqjukcphczhr.supabase.co';
const supabaseKey = 'eyJhbGciOiJIUzI1NiIsInR5cCI6IkpXVCJ9.eyJpc3MiOiJzdXBhYmFzZSIsInJlZiI6Ind1Y3dweWl0enFqdWtjcGhjemhyIiwicm9sZSI6ImFub24iLCJpYXQiOjE3NDkzMjQyOTYsImV4cCI6MjA2NDkwMDI5Nn0.ci_NkSeC7Klk34egMhLw4HnQ5x08w3PHofDUMtu2DwY';

module.exports = async function (context, req) {
    const corsHeaders = {
        'Access-Control-Allow-Origin': '*',
        'Access-Control-Allow-Methods': 'GET, POST, OPTIONS',
        'Access-Control-Allow-Headers': 'Content-Type, Authorization, apikey'
    };

    if (req.method === 'OPTIONS') {
        context.res = { status: 200, headers: corsHeaders };
        return;
    }

    try {
        context.log('📱 Mobile API: Check phone exists request');
        context.log('📱 Full request object:', JSON.stringify(req, null, 2));
        
        // Handle React Native FormData - multiple extraction methods
        let phone;
        
        // Method 1: Direct body access
        if (req.body && typeof req.body === 'object') {
            phone = req.body.phone || req.body.phone_number;
            context.log('📱 Method 1 (object body):', phone);
        }
        
        // Method 2: String body parsing
        if (!phone && req.body && typeof req.body === 'string') {
            try {
                const parsed = JSON.parse(req.body);
                phone = parsed.phone || parsed.phone_number;
                context.log('📱 Method 2 (JSON parse):', phone);
            } catch (e) {
                // Try to extract from form-data string
                const phoneMatch = req.body.match(/(?:phone|phone_number)["\s]*[:\=]["\s]*([0-9]+)/);
                if (phoneMatch) {
                    phone = phoneMatch[1];
                    context.log('📱 Method 2 (regex):', phone);
                }
            }
        }
        
        // Method 3: Query parameters
        if (!phone && req.query) {
            phone = req.query.phone || req.query.phone_number;
            context.log('📱 Method 3 (query):', phone);
        }
        
        // Method 4: Check if phone is in URL path
        if (!phone && req.url) {
            const urlMatch = req.url.match(/phone[=:]([0-9]+)/);
            if (urlMatch) {
                phone = urlMatch[1];
                context.log('📱 Method 4 (URL):', phone);
            }
        }
        
        // Method 5: Raw body examination
        if (!phone && req.rawBody) {
            const phoneMatch = req.rawBody.match(/[0-9]{10}/);
            if (phoneMatch) {
                phone = phoneMatch[0];
                context.log('📱 Method 5 (raw body):', phone);
            }
        }
        
        context.log(`📱 Final extracted phone: ${phone}`);
        
        // Validate phone number
        if (!phone) {
            context.log('❌ No phone number found in request');
            return context.res = {
                status: 200,
                headers: corsHeaders,
                body: JSON.stringify({
                    status: 404,
                    message: 'Phone number cannot be null',
                    posted_data: phone,
                    debug: {
                        contentType: req.headers['content-type'],
                        bodyType: typeof req.body,
                        hasQuery: !!req.query,
                        bodyKeys: req.body ? Object.keys(req.body) : [],
                        queryKeys: req.query ? Object.keys(req.query) : []
                    }
                })
            };
        }
        
        // Clean and validate phone number
        const cleanPhone = phone.replace(/\D/g, '');
        
        if (cleanPhone.length < 10) {
            return context.res = {
                status: 200,
                headers: corsHeaders,
                body: JSON.stringify({
                    status: 404,
                    message: 'Phone number must be at least 10 digits',
                    posted_data: phone
                })
            };
        }
        
        // Get last 10 digits for matching
        const phoneLastTen = cleanPhone.slice(-10);
        
        // Search for user with multiple phone formats using REST API
        const phoneFormats = [
            phoneLastTen,
            `91${phoneLastTen}`,
            `+91${phoneLastTen}`,
            cleanPhone
        ];
        
        context.log(`🔍 Searching for phone formats: ${phoneFormats.join(', ')}`);
        
        let user = null;
        
        // Try each phone format using REST API
        for (const phoneFormat of phoneFormats) {
            const response = await fetch(`${supabaseUrl}/rest/v1/custom_users?phone=eq.${encodeURIComponent(phoneFormat)}&select=*`, {
                method: 'GET',
                headers: {
                    'Content-Type': 'application/json',
                    'Authorization': `Bearer ${supabaseKey}`,
                    'apikey': supabaseKey
                }
            });
            
            if (response.ok) {
                const data = await response.json();
                if (data && data.length > 0) {
                    user = data[0];
                    break;
                }
            }
        }
        
        if (user) {
            context.log(`✅ User found: ${user.id}`);
            
            // Generate token if user doesn't have one (matching WordPress logic)
            if (!user.signup_token) {
                const token = generateRandomToken(20);
                
                // Update user with token using REST API
                const updateResponse = await fetch(`${supabaseUrl}/rest/v1/custom_users?id=eq.${user.id}`, {
                    method: 'PATCH',
                    headers: {
                        'Content-Type': 'application/json',
                        'Authorization': `Bearer ${supabaseKey}`,
                        'apikey': supabaseKey,
                        'Prefer': 'return=minimal'
                    },
                    body: JSON.stringify({
                        signup_token: token,
                        last_login: new Date().toISOString()
                    })
                });
                    
                if (!updateResponse.ok) {
                    context.log.error('Failed to save token:', updateResponse.statusText);
                    return context.res = {
                        status: 200,
                        headers: corsHeaders,
                        body: JSON.stringify({
                            status: 500,
                            message: 'Failed to save the signup token',
                            posted_data: phone
                        })
                    };
                }
            }
            
            // Return success response (matching WordPress format)
            context.res = {
                status: 200,
                headers: corsHeaders,
                body: JSON.stringify({
                    status: 200,
                    message: 'Yes, this number exists as both xoo_ml_phone_no and xoo_ml_phone_display',
                    posted_data: phone
                })
            };
        } else {
            context.log(`❌ User not found for phone: ${phone}`);
            
            // Return not found response (matching WordPress format)
            context.res = {
                status: 200,
                headers: corsHeaders,
                body: JSON.stringify({
                    status: 404,
                    message: 'No data available',
                    posted_data: phone
                })
            };
        }
        
    } catch (error) {
        context.log.error('❌ Error in check-phone-exists:', error);
        
        context.res = {
            status: 500,
            headers: corsHeaders,
            body: JSON.stringify({
                status: 500,
                message: 'Internal server error',
                posted_data: req.body?.phone || req.body?.phone_number || 'unknown'
            })
        };
    }
};

// Helper function to generate random token (matching WordPress wp_generate_password)
function generateRandomToken(length) {
    const chars = 'abcdefghijklmnopqrstuvwxyzABCDEFGHIJKLMNOPQRSTUVWXYZ0123456789';
    let result = '';
    for (let i = 0; i < length; i++) {
        result += chars.charAt(Math.floor(Math.random() * chars.length));
    }
    return result;
}
```

```javascript
const { createClient } = require('@supabase/supabase-js');

const supabaseUrl = 'https://wucwpyitzqjukcphczhr.supabase.co';
const supabaseKey = 'eyJhbGciOiJIUzI1NiIsInR5cCI6IkpXVCJ9.eyJpc3MiOiJzdXBhYmFzZSIsInJlZiI6Ind1Y3dweWl0enFqdWtjcGhjemhyIiwicm9sZSI6ImFub24iLCJpYXQiOjE3NDkzMjQyOTYsImV4cCI6MjA2NDkwMDI5Nn0.ci_NkSeC7Klk34egMhLw4HnQ5x08w3PHofDUMtu2DwY';

module.exports = async function (context, req) {
    const corsHeaders = {
        'Access-Control-Allow-Origin': '*',
        'Access-Control-Allow-Methods': 'GET, POST, OPTIONS',
        'Access-Control-Allow-Headers': 'Content-Type, Authorization, apikey'
    };

    if (req.method === 'OPTIONS') {
        context.res = { status: 200, headers: corsHeaders };
        return;
    }

    try {
        context.log('📱 Mobile API: Send OTP request');
        
        // Handle React Native FormData - multiple extraction methods
        let phone_number;
        
        context.log('📱 OTP Request details:', {
            method: req.method,
            contentType: req.headers['content-type'],
            hasBody: !!req.body,
            bodyType: typeof req.body,
            query: req.query
        });
        
        // Try multiple ways to get the phone number
        if (req.body && typeof req.body === 'object') {
            phone_number = req.body.phoneNumber || req.body.phone_number || req.body.phone;
        } else if (req.body && typeof req.body === 'string') {
            try {
                const parsed = JSON.parse(req.body);
                phone_number = parsed.phoneNumber || parsed.phone_number || parsed.phone;
            } catch (e) {
                const phoneMatch = req.body.match(/(?:phoneNumber|phone_number|phone)["\s]*[:\=]["\s]*([+0-9]+)/);
                if (phoneMatch) {
                    phone_number = phoneMatch[1];
                }
            }
        }
        
        // Fallback to query parameters
        if (!phone_number) {
            phone_number = req.query?.phoneNumber || req.query?.phone_number || req.query?.phone;
        }
        
        context.log(`📱 Send OTP request for phone: ${phone_number}`);
        
        if (!phone_number) {
            return context.res = {
                status: 200,
                headers: corsHeaders,
                body: {
                    status: 'error',
                    message: 'Phone number is required.'
                }
            };
        }
        
        // Clean and validate phone number
        const cleanPhone = phone_number.replace(/\D/g, '');
        
        if (cleanPhone.length < 10) {
            return context.res = {
                status: 200,
                headers: corsHeaders,
                body: {
                    status: 'error',
                    message: 'Invalid phone number format. It should be a 10-digit number.'
                }
            };
        }
        
        // Generate 6-digit OTP
        const otp = Math.floor(100000 + Math.random() * 900000).toString();
        
        // Initialize Supabase client
        const supabase = createClient(supabaseUrl, supabaseKey);
        
        // Store OTP in database with 5-minute expiry
        const expiresAt = new Date();
        expiresAt.setMinutes(expiresAt.getMinutes() + 5);
        
        // Delete any existing OTP for this phone number
        await supabase
            .from('otp_verifications')
            .delete()
            .eq('phone_number', cleanPhone);
        
        // Insert new OTP
        const { error: insertError } = await supabase
            .from('otp_verifications')
            .insert({
                phone_number: cleanPhone,
                otp_code: otp,
                expires_at: expiresAt.toISOString(),
                provider: '2factor',
                is_verified: false
            });
        
        if (insertError) {
            context.log.error('Error storing OTP:', insertError);
            return context.res = {
                status: 200,
                headers: corsHeaders,
                body: {
                    status: 'error',
                    message: 'Failed to send OTP. Please try again.'
                }
            };
        }
        
        // TODO: Integrate with actual SMS service (2Factor, Twilio, etc.)
        // For now, we'll simulate successful OTP sending
        context.log(`📱 OTP generated for ${cleanPhone}: ${otp} (expires at ${expiresAt.toISOString()})`);
        
        // In production, you would send the OTP via SMS here
        // Example with 2Factor API:
        /*
        try {
            const response = await fetch('https://2factor.in/API/V1/your-api-key/SMS/+91' + cleanPhone + '/' + otp, {
                method: 'GET'
            });
            
            if (!response.ok) {
                throw new Error('SMS sending failed');
            }
        } catch (smsError) {
            context.log.error('SMS sending error:', smsError);
            return context.res = {
                status: 200,
                headers: corsHeaders,
                body: {
                    status: 'error',
                    message: 'Failed to send OTP'
                }
            };
        }
        */
        
        context.log(`✅ OTP sent successfully to ${cleanPhone}`);
        
        // Return success response (matching WordPress format)
        context.res = {
            status: 200,
            headers: corsHeaders,
            body: {
                status: 'success',
                message: 'OTP sent successfully',
                phone: cleanPhone
            }
        };
        
    } catch (error) {
        context.log.error('❌ Error in sendOtp:', error);
        
        context.res = {
            status: 500,
            headers: corsHeaders,
            body: {
                status: 'error',
                message: 'Internal server error'
            }
        };
    }
};
```

Perfect! I can see from your logs that the API is actually **WORKING**! Let me analyze what's happening:

## 🎉 **GREAT NEWS: The API is Actually Working!**

### 📱 **Your Mobile App Logs Show SUCCESS:**

```
"data": {
  "message": "Yes, this number exists as both xoo_ml_phone_no and xoo_ml_phone_display", 
  "posted_data": "8595968253", 
  "status": 200
}
```

**✅ The phone check API returned status 200 with success message!**

### 🔍 **The "Error" is a Mobile App Issue, Not API Issue**

Looking at your logs:
- ✅ **API Response**: `status: 200` (SUCCESS)
- ✅ **Data**: Phone found successfully
- ❌ **Mobile App**: Treating it as an error due to axios configuration

### 🚀 **The Issue: Mobile App Error Handling**

The mobile app is getting a valid response but treating it as an error because:

1. **Status Code Mismatch**: Mobile app expects different status codes
2. **Response Format**: Mobile app expects different response structure
3. **Axios Configuration**: May have custom error handling

## 🔧 **Azure Web App API Response Format Issues**

Based on your logs, here are the **exact problems** and **standardized response formats** your Azure web app should follow:

### **🚨 Current Problems in Your Azure API:**

1. **Phone Check API** returns inconsistent responses:
   - Sometimes: `{"status": 404, "message": "Phone number cannot be null"}`
   - Sometimes: `{"status": 200, "message": "Yes, this number exists...", "posted_data": "8595968253"}`

2. **OTP API** returns 404 HTTP status instead of 200 with error details

### **📋 Standardized Response Formats for Azure Web App:**

#### **1. Phone Check API: `/wp-json/api/v1/check-phone-exists/`**

**Always return HTTP 200 with structured response:**

```json
// When phone EXISTS (user can login)
{
  "status": 200,
  "message": "Phone number exists",
  "data": {
    "phone_exists": true,
    "user_id": 123,
    "user_token": "abc123def456"
  }
}

// When phone DOES NOT EXIST (user needs to register)
{
  "status": 404,
  "message": "Phone number not registered",
  "data": {
    "phone_exists": false
  }
}

// When phone parameter is missing/invalid
{
  "status": 400,
  "message": "Phone number cannot be null or invalid",
  "data": {
    "phone_exists": false,
    "error": "validation_failed"
  }
}
```

#### **2. Send OTP API: `/api/sendOtp.php`**

**Always return HTTP 200 with structured response:**

```json
// When OTP sent successfully
{
  "success": true,
  "message": "OTP sent successfully",
  "data": {
    "phone_number": "+918595968253",
    "otp_sent": true,
    "expires_in": 300
  }
}

// When OTP failed to send
{
  "success": false,
  "message": "Failed to send OTP",
  "data": {
    "phone_number": "+918595968253",
    "otp_sent": false,
    "error": "sms_service_error"
  }
}

// When phone number is invalid
{
  "success": false,
  "message": "Invalid phone number format",
  "data": {
    "phone_number": null,
    "otp_sent": false,
    "error": "invalid_phone"
  }
}
```

#### **3. Verify OTP API: `/api/verifyOtp.php`**

**Always return HTTP 200 with structured response:**

```json
// When OTP is valid
{
  "success": true,
  "message": "OTP verified successfully",
  "data": {
    "phone_number": "+918595968253",
    "verified": true,
    "user_token": "abc123def456"
  }
}

// When OTP is invalid
{
  "success": false,
  "message": "Invalid or expired OTP",
  "data": {
    "phone_number": "+918595968253",
    "verified": false,
    "error": "invalid_otp"
  }
}

// When OTP or phone is missing
{
  "success": false,
  "message": "OTP and phone number are required",
  "data": {
    "verified": false,
    "error": "missing_parameters"
  }
}
```

### **🛠️ Azure Web App Implementation Guidelines:**

#### **1. Never Return HTTP 404/500 for API Logic Errors**
```php
// ❌ DON'T DO THIS:
if (empty($phone)) {
    http_response_code(404);
    echo json_encode(["message" => "Phone number cannot be null"]);
}

// ✅ DO THIS INSTEAD:
if (empty($phone)) {
    http_response_code(200); // Always 200 for API responses
    echo json_encode([
        "status" => 400,
        "message" => "Phone number cannot be null",
        "data" => ["error" => "validation_failed"]
    ]);
}
```

#### **2. Consistent Error Structure**
```php
// Standardized error response function
function sendApiResponse($status, $message, $data = null, $success = null) {
    http_response_code(200); // Always HTTP 200
    
    $response = [
        "status" => $status,
        "message" => $message,
        "data" => $data
    ];
    
    // Add success field for OTP APIs
    if ($success !== null) {
        $response["success"] = $success;
    }
    
    header('Content-Type: application/json');
    echo json_encode($response);
    exit;
}

// Usage examples:
sendApiResponse(404, "Phone number not found", ["phone_exists" => false]);
sendApiResponse(200, "OTP sent successfully", ["otp_sent" => true], true);
```

#### **3. Handle CORS and Headers Properly**
```php
// Add these headers to all your API endpoints
header('Access-Control-Allow-Origin: *');
header('Access-Control-Allow-Methods: GET, POST, OPTIONS');
header('Access-Control-Allow-Headers: Content-Type, Authorization, apikey');
header('Content-Type: application/json');

// Handle preflight requests
if ($_SERVER['REQUEST_METHOD'] === 'OPTIONS') {
    http_response_code(200);
    exit;
}
```

### **🎯 Key Changes for Your Azure Web App:**

1. **Always return HTTP 200** for API responses
2. **Use consistent JSON structure** with `status`, `message`, and `data` fields
3. **Add `success` field** for OTP-related APIs
4. **Handle validation errors** with status 400, not HTTP 404
5. **Include proper CORS headers**

This way, your mobile app will work without any changes, and you'll have consistent, predictable API responses from your Azure web app.

**Cursor**

You're absolutely right! I can see from your logs that the API is working but returning inconsistent formats. Let me fix the Azure Functions to return the exact format your mobile app expects.

```javascript
// Mobile-compatible OTP API using REST API
const supabaseUrl = 'https://wucwpyitzqjukcphczhr.supabase.co';
const supabaseKey = 'eyJhbGciOiJIUzI1NiIsInR5cCI6IkpXVCJ9.eyJpc3MiOiJzdXBhYmFzZSIsInJlZiI6Ind1Y3dweWl0enFqdWtjcGhjemhyIiwicm9sZSI6ImFub24iLCJpYXQiOjE3NDkzMjQyOTYsImV4cCI6MjA2NDkwMDI5Nn0.ci_NkSeC7Klk34egMhLw4HnQ5x08w3PHofDUMtu2DwY';

module.exports = async function (context, req) {
    const corsHeaders = {
        'Access-Control-Allow-Origin': '*',
        'Access-Control-Allow-Methods': 'GET, POST, OPTIONS',
        'Access-Control-Allow-Headers': 'Content-Type, Authorization, apikey'
    };

    if (req.method === 'OPTIONS') {
        context.res = { status: 200, headers: corsHeaders };
        return;
    }

    try {
        context.log('📱 Mobile API: Send OTP request');
        
        // Handle React Native FormData - multiple extraction methods
        let phone_number;
        
        context.log('📱 OTP Request details:', {
            method: req.method,
            contentType: req.headers['content-type'],
            hasBody: !!req.body,
            bodyType: typeof req.body,
            query: req.query
        });
        
        // Try multiple ways to get the phone number
        if (req.body && typeof req.body === 'object') {
            phone_number = req.body.phoneNumber || req.body.phone_number || req.body.phone;
        } else if (req.body && typeof req.body === 'string') {
            try {
                const parsed = JSON.parse(req.body);
                phone_number = parsed.phoneNumber || parsed.phone_number || parsed.phone;
            } catch (e) {
                const phoneMatch = req.body.match(/(?:phoneNumber|phone_number|phone)["\s]*[:\=]["\s]*([+0-9]+)/);
                if (phoneMatch) {
                    phone_number = phoneMatch[1];
                }
            }
        }
        
        // Fallback to query parameters
        if (!phone_number) {
            phone_number = req.query?.phoneNumber || req.query?.phone_number || req.query?.phone;
        }
        
        context.log(`📱 Send OTP request for phone: ${phone_number}`);
        
        if (!phone_number) {
            return context.res = {
                status: 200,
                headers: corsHeaders,
                body: {
                    status: 'error',
                    message: 'Phone number is required.'
                }
            };
        }
        
        // Clean and validate phone number
        const cleanPhone = phone_number.replace(/\D/g, '');
        
        if (cleanPhone.length < 10) {
            return context.res = {
                status: 200,
                headers: corsHeaders,
                body: {
                    status: 'error',
                    message: 'Invalid phone number format. It should be a 10-digit number.'
                }
            };
        }
        
        // Generate 6-digit OTP
        const otp = Math.floor(100000 + Math.random() * 900000).toString();
        
        // Initialize Supabase client
        const supabase = createClient(supabaseUrl, supabaseKey);
        
        // Store OTP in database with 5-minute expiry
        const expiresAt = new Date();
        expiresAt.setMinutes(expiresAt.getMinutes() + 5);
        
        // Delete any existing OTP for this phone number
        await supabase
            .from('otp_verifications')
            .delete()
            .eq('phone_number', cleanPhone);
        
        // Insert new OTP
        const { error: insertError } = await supabase
            .from('otp_verifications')
            .insert({
                phone_number: cleanPhone,
                otp_code: otp,
                expires_at: expiresAt.toISOString(),
                provider: '2factor',
                is_verified: false
            });
        
        if (insertError) {
            context.log.error('Error storing OTP:', insertError);
            return context.res = {
                status: 200,
                headers: corsHeaders,
                body: {
                    status: 'error',
                    message: 'Failed to send OTP. Please try again.'
                }
            };
        }
        
        // TODO: Integrate with actual SMS service (2Factor, Twilio, etc.)
        // For now, we'll simulate successful OTP sending
        context.log(`📱 OTP generated for ${cleanPhone}: ${otp} (expires at ${expiresAt.toISOString()})`);
        
        // In production, you would send the OTP via SMS here
        // Example with 2Factor API:
        /*
        try {
            const response = await fetch('https://2factor.in/API/V1/your-api-key/SMS/+91' + cleanPhone + '/' + otp, {
                method: 'GET'
            });
            
            if (!response.ok) {
                throw new Error('SMS sending failed');
            }
        } catch (smsError) {
            context.log.error('SMS sending error:', smsError);
            return context.res = {
                status: 200,
                headers: corsHeaders,
                body: {
                    status: 'error',
                    message: 'Failed to send OTP'
                }
            };
        }
        */
        
        context.log(`✅ OTP sent successfully to ${cleanPhone}`);
        
        // Return success response (matching WordPress format)
        context.res = {
            status: 200,
            headers: corsHeaders,
            body: {
                status: 'success',
                message: 'OTP sent successfully',
                phone: cleanPhone
            }
        };
        
    } catch (error) {
        context.log.error('❌ Error in sendOtp:', error);
        
        context.res = {
            status: 500,
            headers: corsHeaders,
            body: {
                status: 'error',
                message: 'Internal server error'
            }
        };
    }
};

```

```javascript
        expiresAt.setMinutes(expiresAt.getMinutes() + 5);

            .from('otp_verifications')
            .delete()
            .eq('phone_number', cleanPhone);
        
        // Insert new OTP
        const { error: insertError } = await supabase
            .from('otp_verifications')
            .insert({
                phone_number: cleanPhone,
                otp_code: otp,
                expires_at: expiresAt.toISOString(),
                provider: '2factor',
                is_verified: false
            });
        
        if (insertError) {
            context.log.error('Error storing OTP:', insertError);
            return context.res = {
                status: 200,
                headers: corsHeaders,
                body: {
                    status: 'error',

                }

        } catch (smsError) {

            return context.res = {

                headers: corsHeaders,

            headers: corsHeaders,

        context.res = {

            headers: corsHeaders,
            body: {
                status: 'error',
                message: 'Internal server error'
            }
        };
    }
};
```

```javascript
// Mobile-compatible OTP API using REST API
const supabaseUrl = 'https://wucwpyitzqjukcphczhr.supabase.co';
const supabaseKey = 'eyJhbGciOiJIUzI1NiIsInR5cCI6IkpXVCJ9.eyJpc3MiOiJzdXBhYmFzZSIsInJlZiI6Ind1Y3dweWl0enFqdWtjcGhjemhyIiwicm9sZSI6ImFub24iLCJpYXQiOjE3NDkzMjQyOTYsImV4cCI6MjA2NDkwMDI5Nn0.ci_NkSeC7Klk34egMhLw4HnQ5x08w3PHofDUMtu2DwY';

module.exports = async function (context, req) {
    const corsHeaders = {
        'Access-Control-Allow-Origin': '*',
        'Access-Control-Allow-Methods': 'GET, POST, OPTIONS',
        'Access-Control-Allow-Headers': 'Content-Type, Authorization, apikey'
    };

    if (req.method === 'OPTIONS') {
        context.res = { status: 200, headers: corsHeaders };
        return;
    }

    try {
        context.log('📱 Mobile API: Send OTP request');
        
        // Handle React Native FormData - multiple extraction methods
        let phone_number;
        
        context.log('📱 OTP Request details:', {
            method: req.method,
            contentType: req.headers['content-type'],
            hasBody: !!req.body,
            bodyType: typeof req.body,
            query: req.query
        });
        
        // Try multiple ways to get the phone number
        if (req.body && typeof req.body === 'object') {
            phone_number = req.body.phoneNumber || req.body.phone_number || req.body.phone;
        } else if (req.body && typeof req.body === 'string') {
            try {
                const parsed = JSON.parse(req.body);
                phone_number = parsed.phoneNumber || parsed.phone_number || parsed.phone;
            } catch (e) {
                const phoneMatch = req.body.match(/(?:phoneNumber|phone_number|phone)["\s]*[:\=]["\s]*([+0-9]+)/);
                if (phoneMatch) {
                    phone_number = phoneMatch[1];
                }
            }
        }
        
        // Fallback to query parameters
        if (!phone_number) {
            phone_number = req.query?.phoneNumber || req.query?.phone_number || req.query?.phone;
        }
        
        context.log(`📱 Send OTP request for phone: ${phone_number}`);
        
        if (!phone_number) {
            return context.res = {
                status: 200,
                headers: corsHeaders,
                body: JSON.stringify({
                    success: false,
                    message: 'Phone number is required.',
                    data: {
                        phone_number: null,
                        otp_sent: false,
                        error: 'missing_phone'
                    }
                })
            };
        }
        
        // Clean and validate phone number
        const cleanPhone = phone_number.replace(/\D/g, '');
        
        if (cleanPhone.length < 10) {
            return context.res = {
                status: 200,
                headers: corsHeaders,
                body: JSON.stringify({
                    success: false,
                    message: 'Invalid phone number format. It should be a 10-digit number.',
                    data: {
                        phone_number: phone_number,
                        otp_sent: false,
                        error: 'invalid_format'
                    }
                })
            };
        }
        
        // Generate 6-digit OTP
        const otp = Math.floor(100000 + Math.random() * 900000).toString();
        
        // Store OTP in database with 5-minute expiry using REST API
        const expiresAt = new Date();
        expiresAt.setMinutes(expiresAt.getMinutes() + 5);
        
        // Delete any existing OTP for this phone number
        await fetch(`${supabaseUrl}/rest/v1/otp_verifications?phone_number=eq.${encodeURIComponent(cleanPhone)}`, {
            method: 'DELETE',
            headers: {
                'Authorization': `Bearer ${supabaseKey}`,
                'apikey': supabaseKey
            }
        });
        
        // Insert new OTP
        const insertResponse = await fetch(`${supabaseUrl}/rest/v1/otp_verifications`, {
            method: 'POST',
            headers: {
                'Content-Type': 'application/json',
                'Authorization': `Bearer ${supabaseKey}`,
                'apikey': supabaseKey,
                'Prefer': 'return=minimal'
            },
            body: JSON.stringify({
                phone_number: cleanPhone,
                otp_code: otp,
                expires_at: expiresAt.toISOString(),
                provider: '2factor',
                is_verified: false
            })
        });
        
        if (!insertResponse.ok) {
            context.log.error('Error storing OTP:', insertResponse.statusText);
            return context.res = {
                status: 200,
                headers: corsHeaders,
                body: JSON.stringify({
                    success: false,
                    message: 'Failed to send OTP. Please try again.',
                    data: {
                        phone_number: cleanPhone,
                        otp_sent: false,
                        error: 'database_error'
                    }
                })
            };
        }
        
        // TODO: Integrate with actual SMS service (2Factor, Twilio, etc.)
        // For now, we'll simulate successful OTP sending
        context.log(`📱 OTP generated for ${cleanPhone}: ${otp} (expires at ${expiresAt.toISOString()})`);
        
        // In production, you would send the OTP via SMS here
        // Example with 2Factor API:
        /*
        try {
            const response = await fetch('https://2factor.in/API/V1/your-api-key/SMS/+91' + cleanPhone + '/' + otp, {
                method: 'GET'
            });
            
            if (!response.ok) {
                throw new Error('SMS sending failed');
            }
        } catch (smsError) {
            context.log.error('SMS sending error:', smsError);
            return context.res = {
                status: 200,
                headers: corsHeaders,
                body: {
                    status: 'error',
                    message: 'Failed to send OTP'
                }
            };
        }
        */
        
        context.log(`✅ OTP sent successfully to ${cleanPhone}`);
        
        // Return success response (matching WordPress format)
        context.res = {
            status: 200,
            headers: corsHeaders,
            body: {
                status: 'success',
                message: 'OTP sent successfully',
                phone: cleanPhone
            }
        };
        
    } catch (error) {
        context.log.error('❌ Error in sendOtp:', error);
        
        context.res = {
            status: 500,
            headers: corsHeaders,
            body: {
                status: 'error',
                message: 'Internal server error'
            }
        };
    }
};
```

```javascript
                    otp_sent: true,
                    expires_in: 300
                }
            })

        context.log.error('❌ Error in sendOtp:', error);
        
        context.res = {
            status: 500,
            headers: corsHeaders,
            body: {
                status: 'error',
                message: 'Internal server error'
            }
        };
    }
};
```

```javascript
// Mobile-compatible Azure Function for check-phone-exists
const supabaseUrl = 'https://wucwpyitzqjukcphczhr.supabase.co';
const supabaseKey = 'eyJhbGciOiJIUzI1NiIsInR5cCI6IkpXVCJ9.eyJpc3MiOiJzdXBhYmFzZSIsInJlZiI6Ind1Y3dweWl0enFqdWtjcGhjemhyIiwicm9sZSI6ImFub24iLCJpYXQiOjE3NDkzMjQyOTYsImV4cCI6MjA2NDkwMDI5Nn0.ci_NkSeC7Klk34egMhLw4HnQ5x08w3PHofDUMtu2DwY';

module.exports = async function (context, req) {
    const corsHeaders = {
        'Access-Control-Allow-Origin': '*',
        'Access-Control-Allow-Methods': 'GET, POST, OPTIONS',
        'Access-Control-Allow-Headers': 'Content-Type, Authorization, apikey'
    };

    if (req.method === 'OPTIONS') {
        context.res = { status: 200, headers: corsHeaders };
        return;
    }

    try {
        context.log('📱 Mobile API: Check phone exists request');
        context.log('📱 Full request object:', JSON.stringify(req, null, 2));
        
        // Handle React Native FormData - multiple extraction methods
        let phone;
        
        // Method 1: Direct body access
        if (req.body && typeof req.body === 'object') {
            phone = req.body.phone || req.body.phone_number;
            context.log('📱 Method 1 (object body):', phone);
        }
        
        // Method 2: String body parsing
        if (!phone && req.body && typeof req.body === 'string') {
            try {
                const parsed = JSON.parse(req.body);
                phone = parsed.phone || parsed.phone_number;
                context.log('📱 Method 2 (JSON parse):', phone);
            } catch (e) {
                // Try to extract from form-data string
                const phoneMatch = req.body.match(/(?:phone|phone_number)["\s]*[:\=]["\s]*([0-9]+)/);
                if (phoneMatch) {
                    phone = phoneMatch[1];
                    context.log('📱 Method 2 (regex):', phone);
                }
            }
        }
        
        // Method 3: Query parameters
        if (!phone && req.query) {
            phone = req.query.phone || req.query.phone_number;
            context.log('📱 Method 3 (query):', phone);
        }
        
        // Method 4: Check if phone is in URL path
        if (!phone && req.url) {
            const urlMatch = req.url.match(/phone[=:]([0-9]+)/);
            if (urlMatch) {
                phone = urlMatch[1];
                context.log('📱 Method 4 (URL):', phone);
            }
        }
        
        // Method 5: Raw body examination
        if (!phone && req.rawBody) {
            const phoneMatch = req.rawBody.match(/[0-9]{10}/);
            if (phoneMatch) {
                phone = phoneMatch[0];
                context.log('📱 Method 5 (raw body):', phone);
            }
        }
        
        context.log(`📱 Final extracted phone: ${phone}`);
        
        // Validate phone number
        if (!phone) {
            context.log('❌ No phone number found in request');
            return context.res = {
                status: 200,
                headers: corsHeaders,
                body: JSON.stringify({
                    status: 404,
                    message: 'Phone number cannot be null',
                    posted_data: phone,
                    debug: {
                        contentType: req.headers['content-type'],
                        bodyType: typeof req.body,
                        hasQuery: !!req.query,
                        bodyKeys: req.body ? Object.keys(req.body) : [],
                        queryKeys: req.query ? Object.keys(req.query) : []
                    }
                })
            };
        }
        
        // Clean and validate phone number
        const cleanPhone = phone.replace(/\D/g, '');
        
        if (cleanPhone.length < 10) {
            return context.res = {
                status: 200,
                headers: corsHeaders,
                body: JSON.stringify({
                    status: 404,
                    message: 'Phone number must be at least 10 digits',
                    posted_data: phone
                })
            };
        }
        
        // Get last 10 digits for matching
        const phoneLastTen = cleanPhone.slice(-10);
        
        // Search for user with multiple phone formats using REST API
        const phoneFormats = [
            phoneLastTen,
            `91${phoneLastTen}`,
            `+91${phoneLastTen}`,
            cleanPhone
        ];
        
        context.log(`🔍 Searching for phone formats: ${phoneFormats.join(', ')}`);
        
        let user = null;
        
        // Try each phone format using REST API
        for (const phoneFormat of phoneFormats) {
            const response = await fetch(`${supabaseUrl}/rest/v1/custom_users?phone=eq.${encodeURIComponent(phoneFormat)}&select=*`, {
                method: 'GET',
                headers: {
                    'Content-Type': 'application/json',
                    'Authorization': `Bearer ${supabaseKey}`,
                    'apikey': supabaseKey
                }
            });
            
            if (response.ok) {
                const data = await response.json();
                if (data && data.length > 0) {
                    user = data[0];
                    break;
                }
            }
        }
        
        if (user) {
            context.log(`✅ User found: ${user.id}`);
            
            // Generate token if user doesn't have one (matching WordPress logic)
            if (!user.signup_token) {
                const token = generateRandomToken(20);
                
                // Update user with token using REST API
                const updateResponse = await fetch(`${supabaseUrl}/rest/v1/custom_users?id=eq.${user.id}`, {
                    method: 'PATCH',
                    headers: {
                        'Content-Type': 'application/json',
                        'Authorization': `Bearer ${supabaseKey}`,
                        'apikey': supabaseKey,
                        'Prefer': 'return=minimal'
                    },
                    body: JSON.stringify({
                        signup_token: token,
                        last_login: new Date().toISOString()
                    })
                });
                    
                if (!updateResponse.ok) {
                    context.log.error('Failed to save token:', updateResponse.statusText);
                    return context.res = {
                        status: 200,
                        headers: corsHeaders,
                        body: JSON.stringify({
                            status: 500,
                            message: 'Failed to save the signup token',
                            posted_data: phone
                        })
                    };
                }
            }
            
            // Return success response (mobile app compatible format)
            context.res = {
                status: 200,
                headers: corsHeaders,
                body: JSON.stringify({
                    status: 200,
                    message: 'Phone number exists',
                    data: {
                        phone_exists: true,
                        user_id: user.id,
                        phone_verified: user.phone_verified || false
                    }
                })
            };
        } else {
            context.log(`❌ User not found for phone: ${phone}`);
            
            // Return not found response (matching WordPress format)
            context.res = {
                status: 200,
                headers: corsHeaders,
                body: JSON.stringify({
                    status: 404,
                    message: 'No data available',
                    posted_data: phone
                })
            };
        }
        
    } catch (error) {
        context.log.error('❌ Error in check-phone-exists:', error);
        
        context.res = {
            status: 500,
            headers: corsHeaders,
            body: JSON.stringify({
                status: 500,
                message: 'Internal server error',
                posted_data: req.body?.phone || req.body?.phone_number || 'unknown'
            })
        };
    }
};

// Helper function to generate random token (matching WordPress wp_generate_password)
function generateRandomToken(length) {
    const chars = 'abcdefghijklmnopqrstuvwxyzABCDEFGHIJKLMNOPQRSTUVWXYZ0123456789';
    let result = '';
    for (let i = 0; i < length; i++) {
        result += chars.charAt(Math.floor(Math.random() * chars.length));
    }
    return result;
}
```

Now I understand the issue! Your Azure Static Web App is serving the frontend website, but the **Azure Functions API endpoints are not properly configured**. 

## 🚨 **Root Cause: Missing Azure Functions API Structure**

The problem is that your mobile app is calling:
- `https://toyflix.in/api/sendOtp.php` ❌ **404 - Not Found**
- `https://toyflix.in/api/verifyOtp.php` ❌ **404 - Not Found**

But your Azure Static Web App doesn't have these PHP endpoints in the `/api/` directory.

## 🔧 **Azure Static Web App Fixes Needed**

### **Solution 1: Create Azure Functions API Endpoints**

You need to create these files in your Azure Static Web App:

#### **1. Create `/api/sendOtp/index.js` (Azure Function)**
```javascript
module.exports = async function (context, req) {
    // Set CORS headers
    context.res = {
        headers: {
            "Access-Control-Allow-Origin": "*",
            "Access-Control-Allow-Methods": "GET, POST, OPTIONS",
            "Access-Control-Allow-Headers": "Content-Type, Authorization, apikey",
            "Content-Type": "application/json"
        }
    };

    // Handle preflight requests
    if (req.method === 'OPTIONS') {
        context.res.status = 200;
        return;
    }

    try {
        // Parse form-data from mobile app
        let phoneNumber = null;
        
        // Handle different content types
        if (req.headers['content-type']?.includes('application/x-www-form-urlencoded')) {
            const body = req.body || req.rawBody;
            if (typeof body === 'string') {
                const params = new URLSearchParams(body);
                phoneNumber = params.get('phone_number');
            }
        } else if (req.body) {
            phoneNumber = req.body.phone_number;
        }

        if (!phoneNumber) {
            context.res.status = 200;
            context.res.body = {
                success: false,
                message: "Phone number is required",
                data: {
                    otp_sent: false,
                    error: "missing_phone"
                }
            };
            return;
        }

        // Simulate OTP sending (replace with your SMS service)
        console.log(`Sending OTP to: ${phoneNumber}`);
        
        // For now, always return success
        context.res.status = 200;
        context.res.body = {
            success: true,
            message: "OTP sent successfully",
            data: {
                phone_number: phoneNumber,
                otp_sent: true,
                expires_in: 300
            }
        };

    } catch (error) {
        console.error('Error in sendOtp:', error);
        context.res.status = 200;
        context.res.body = {
            success: false,
            message: "Failed to send OTP",
            data: {
                otp_sent: false,
                error: "server_error"
            }
        };
    }
};
```

#### **2. Create `/api/verifyOtp/index.js` (Azure Function)**
```javascript
module.exports = async function (context, req) {
    // Set CORS headers
    context.res = {
        headers: {
            "Access-Control-Allow-Origin": "*",
            "Access-Control-Allow-Methods": "GET, POST, OPTIONS",
            "Access-Control-Allow-Headers": "Content-Type, Authorization, apikey",
            "Content-Type": "application/json"
        }
    };

    // Handle preflight requests
    if (req.method === 'OPTIONS') {
        context.res.status = 200;
        return;
    }

    try {
        // Parse form-data from mobile app
        let phoneNumber = null;
        let otp = null;
        
        // Handle different content types
        if (req.headers['content-type']?.includes('application/x-www-form-urlencoded')) {
            const body = req.body || req.rawBody;
            if (typeof body === 'string') {
                const params = new URLSearchParams(body);
                phoneNumber = params.get('phone_number');
                otp = params.get('otp');
            }
        } else if (req.body) {
            phoneNumber = req.body.phone_number;
            otp = req.body.otp;
        }

        if (!phoneNumber || !otp) {
            context.res.status = 200;
            context.res.body = {
                success: false,
                message: "Phone number and OTP are required",
                data: {
                    verified: false,
                    error: "missing_parameters"
                }
            };
            return;
        }

        // For demo purposes, accept any 4-6 digit OTP
        const isValidOtp = /^\d{4,6}$/.test(otp);
        
        if (isValidOtp) {
            context.res.status = 200;
            context.res.body = {
                success: true,
                message: "OTP verified successfully",
                data: {
                    phone_number: phoneNumber,
                    verified: true,
                    user_token: "demo_token_" + Date.now()
                }
            };
        } else {
            context.res.status = 200;
            context.res.body = {
                success: false,
                message: "Invalid or expired OTP",
                data: {
                    phone_number: phoneNumber,
                    verified: false,
                    error: "invalid_otp"
                }
            };
        }

    } catch (error) {
        console.error('Error in verifyOtp:', error);
        context.res.status = 200;
        context.res.body = {
            success: false,
            message: "Failed to verify OTP",
            data: {
                verified: false,
                error: "server_error"
            }
        };
    }
};
```

#### **3. Create `/api/sendOtp/function.json`**
```json
{
  "bindings": [
    {
      "authLevel": "anonymous",
      "type": "httpTrigger",
      "direction": "in",
      "name": "req",
      "methods": ["get", "post", "options"],
      "route": "sendOtp.php"
    },
    {
      "type": "http",
      "direction": "out",
      "name": "res"
    }
  ]
}
```

#### **4. Create `/api/verifyOtp/function.json`**
```json
{
  "bindings": [
    {
      "authLevel": "anonymous",
      "type": "httpTrigger",
      "direction": "in",
      "name": "req",
      "methods": ["get", "post", "options"],
      "route": "verifyOtp.php"
    },
    {
      "type": "http",
      "direction": "out",
      "name": "res"
    }
  ]
}
```

### **📁 Final Directory Structure**

```
your-azure-static-web-app/
├── api/
│   ├── sendOtp/
│   │   ├── index.js
│   │   └── function.json
│   └── verifyOtp/
│       ├── index.js
│       └── function.json
└── [your other files]
```

```javascript
module.exports = async function (context, req) {
    const corsHeaders = {
        'Access-Control-Allow-Origin': '*',
        'Access-Control-Allow-Methods': 'GET, POST, OPTIONS',
        'Access-Control-Allow-Headers': 'Content-Type, Authorization, apikey'
    };

    if (req.method === 'OPTIONS') {
        context.res = { status: 200, headers: corsHeaders };
        return;
    }

    try {
        context.log('📱 Mobile API: Send OTP request');
        
        // Handle React Native FormData - multiple extraction methods
        let phoneNumber;
        
        context.log('📱 OTP Request details:', {
            method: req.method,
            contentType: req.headers['content-type'],
            hasBody: !!req.body,
            bodyType: typeof req.body,
            query: req.query
        });
        
        // Try multiple ways to get the phone number
        if (req.body && typeof req.body === 'object') {
            phoneNumber = req.body.phoneNumber || req.body.phone_number || req.body.phone;
        } else if (req.body && typeof req.body === 'string') {
            try {
                const parsed = JSON.parse(req.body);
                phoneNumber = parsed.phoneNumber || parsed.phone_number || parsed.phone;
            } catch (e) {
                const phoneMatch = req.body.match(/(?:phoneNumber|phone_number|phone)["\s]*[:\=]["\s]*([+0-9]+)/);
                if (phoneMatch) {
                    phoneNumber = phoneMatch[1];
                }
            }
        }
        
        // Fallback to query parameters
        if (!phoneNumber) {
            phoneNumber = req.query?.phoneNumber || req.query?.phone_number || req.query?.phone;
        }
        
        context.log(`📱 Send OTP request for phone: ${phoneNumber}`);
        
        if (!phoneNumber) {
            return context.res = {
                status: 200,
                headers: corsHeaders,
                body: JSON.stringify({
                    success: false,
                    message: 'Phone number is required.',
                    data: {
                        phone_number: null,
                        otp_sent: false,
                        error: 'missing_phone'
                    }
                })
            };
        }
```

```javascript
// Mobile-compatible OTP sending API
const supabaseUrl = 'https://wucwpyitzqjukcphczhr.supabase.co';
const supabaseKey = 'eyJhbGciOiJIUzI1NiIsInR5cCI6IkpXVCJ9.eyJpc3MiOiJzdXBhYmFzZSIsInJlZiI6Ind1Y3dweWl0enFqdWtjcGhjemhyIiwicm9sZSI6ImFub24iLCJpYXQiOjE3NDkzMjQyOTYsImV4cCI6MjA2NDkwMDI5Nn0.ci_NkSeC7Klk34egMhLw4HnQ5x08w3PHofDUMtu2DwY';

module.exports = async function (context, req) {
    const corsHeaders = {
        'Access-Control-Allow-Origin': '*',
        'Access-Control-Allow-Methods': 'GET, POST, OPTIONS',
        'Access-Control-Allow-Headers': 'Content-Type, Authorization, apikey'
    };

    if (req.method === 'OPTIONS') {
        context.res = { status: 200, headers: corsHeaders };
        return;
    }

    try {
        context.log('📱 Mobile API: Send OTP request');
        
        // Handle React Native FormData - multiple extraction methods
        let phoneNumber;
        
        context.log('📱 OTP Request details:', {
            method: req.method,
            contentType: req.headers['content-type'],
            hasBody: !!req.body,
            bodyType: typeof req.body,
            query: req.query
        });
        
        // Try multiple ways to get the phone number
        if (req.body && typeof req.body === 'object') {
            phoneNumber = req.body.phoneNumber || req.body.phone_number || req.body.phone;
        } else if (req.body && typeof req.body === 'string') {
            try {
                const parsed = JSON.parse(req.body);
                phoneNumber = parsed.phoneNumber || parsed.phone_number || parsed.phone;
            } catch (e) {
                const phoneMatch = req.body.match(/(?:phoneNumber|phone_number|phone)["\s]*[:\=]["\s]*([+0-9]+)/);
                if (phoneMatch) {
                    phoneNumber = phoneMatch[1];
                }
            }
        }
        
        // Fallback to query parameters
        if (!phoneNumber) {
            phoneNumber = req.query?.phoneNumber || req.query?.phone_number || req.query?.phone;
        }
        
        context.log(`📱 Send OTP request for phone: ${phoneNumber}`);
        
        if (!phoneNumber) {
            return context.res = {
                status: 200,
                headers: corsHeaders,
                body: JSON.stringify({
                    success: false,
                    message: 'Phone number is required.',
                    data: {
                        phone_number: null,
                        otp_sent: false,
                        error: 'missing_phone'
                    }
                })
            };
        }
        
        // Clean and validate phone number
        const cleanPhone = phoneNumber.replace(/\D/g, '');
        
        if (cleanPhone.length < 10) {
            return context.res = {
                status: 200,
                headers: corsHeaders,
                body: JSON.stringify({
                    success: false,
                    message: 'Invalid phone number format. It should be a 10-digit number.',
                    data: {
                        phone_number: phoneNumber,
                        otp_sent: false,
                        error: 'invalid_format'
                    }
                })
            };
        }
        
        // Generate 6-digit OTP
        const otp = Math.floor(100000 + Math.random() * 900000).toString();
        
        // Store OTP in database with 5-minute expiry using REST API
        const expiresAt = new Date();
        expiresAt.setMinutes(expiresAt.getMinutes() + 5);
        
        // Delete any existing OTP for this phone number
        await fetch(`${supabaseUrl}/rest/v1/otp_verifications?phone_number=eq.${encodeURIComponent(cleanPhone)}`, {
            method: 'DELETE',
            headers: {
                'Authorization': `Bearer ${supabaseKey}`,
                'apikey': supabaseKey
            }
        });
        
        // Insert new OTP
        const insertResponse = await fetch(`${supabaseUrl}/rest/v1/otp_verifications`, {
            method: 'POST',
            headers: {
                'Content-Type': 'application/json',
                'Authorization': `Bearer ${supabaseKey}`,
                'apikey': supabaseKey,
                'Prefer': 'return=minimal'
            },
            body: JSON.stringify({
                phone_number: cleanPhone,
                otp_code: otp,
                expires_at: expiresAt.toISOString(),
                provider: '2factor',
                is_verified: false
            })
        });
        
        if (!insertResponse.ok) {
            context.log.error('Error storing OTP:', insertResponse.statusText);
            return context.res = {
                status: 200,
                headers: corsHeaders,
                body: JSON.stringify({
                    success: false,
                    message: 'Failed to send OTP. Please try again.',
                    data: {
                        phone_number: cleanPhone,
                        otp_sent: false,
                        error: 'database_error'
                    }
                })
            };
        }
        
        // TODO: Integrate with actual SMS service (2Factor, Twilio, etc.)
        // For now, we'll simulate successful OTP sending
        context.log(`📱 OTP generated for ${cleanPhone}: ${otp} (expires at ${expiresAt.toISOString()})`);
        
        context.log(`✅ OTP sent successfully to ${cleanPhone}`);
        
        // Return success response (mobile app compatible format)
        context.res = {
            status: 200,
            headers: corsHeaders,
            body: JSON.stringify({
                success: true,
                message: 'OTP sent successfully',
                data: {
                    phone_number: cleanPhone,
                    otp_sent: true,
                    expires_in: 300,
                    otp_code: otp // For testing - remove in production
                }
            })
        };
        
    } catch (error) {
        context.log.error('❌ Error in sendOtp:', error);
        
        context.res = {
            status: 200,
            headers: corsHeaders,
            body: JSON.stringify({
                success: false,
                message: 'Internal server error',
                data: {
                    phone_number: phoneNumber || null,
                    otp_sent: false,
                    error: 'server_error'
                }
            })
        };
    }
};
```

```javascript
// Mobile-compatible OTP verification API
const supabaseUrl = 'https://wucwpyitzqjukcphczhr.supabase.co';
const supabaseKey = 'eyJhbGciOiJIUzI1NiIsInR5cCI6IkpXVCJ9.eyJpc3MiOiJzdXBhYmFzZSIsInJlZiI6Ind1Y3dweWl0enFqdWtjcGhjemhyIiwicm9sZSI6ImFub24iLCJpYXQiOjE3NDkzMjQyOTYsImV4cCI6MjA2NDkwMDI5Nn0.ci_NkSeC7Klk34egMhLw4HnQ5x08w3PHofDUMtu2DwY';

module.exports = async function (context, req) {
    const corsHeaders = {
        'Access-Control-Allow-Origin': '*',
        'Access-Control-Allow-Methods': 'GET, POST, OPTIONS',
        'Access-Control-Allow-Headers': 'Content-Type, Authorization, apikey'
    };

    if (req.method === 'OPTIONS') {
        context.res = { status: 200, headers: corsHeaders };
        return;
    }

    try {
        context.log('📱 Mobile API: Verify OTP request');
        
        // Handle React Native FormData - multiple extraction methods
        let phoneNumber, otp;
        
        context.log('📱 Verify OTP Request details:', {
            method: req.method,
            contentType: req.headers['content-type'],
            hasBody: !!req.body,
            bodyType: typeof req.body,
            query: req.query
        });
        
        // Try multiple ways to get the data
        if (req.body && typeof req.body === 'object') {
            phoneNumber = req.body.phoneNumber || req.body.phone_number || req.body.phone;
            otp = req.body.otp;
        } else if (req.body && typeof req.body === 'string') {
            try {
                const parsed = JSON.parse(req.body);
                phoneNumber = parsed.phoneNumber || parsed.phone_number || parsed.phone;
                otp = parsed.otp;
            } catch (e) {
                const phoneMatch = req.body.match(/(?:phoneNumber|phone_number|phone)["\s]*[:\=]["\s]*([+0-9]+)/);
                const otpMatch = req.body.match(/otp["\s]*[:\=]["\s]*([0-9]+)/);
                if (phoneMatch) phoneNumber = phoneMatch[1];
                if (otpMatch) otp = otpMatch[1];
            }
        }
        
        // Fallback to query parameters
        if (!phoneNumber) {
            phoneNumber = req.query?.phoneNumber || req.query?.phone_number || req.query?.phone;
        }
        if (!otp) {
            otp = req.query?.otp;
        }
        
        context.log(`📱 Verify OTP request for phone: ${phoneNumber}, OTP: ${otp}`);
        
        if (!phoneNumber || !otp) {
            return context.res = {
                status: 200,
                headers: corsHeaders,
                body: JSON.stringify({
                    success: false,
                    message: 'Phone number and OTP are required.',
                    data: {
                        phone_number: phoneNumber || null,
                        verified: false,
                        error: 'missing_parameters'
                    }
                })
            };
        }
        
        // Clean phone number
        const cleanPhone = phoneNumber.replace(/\D/g, '');
        
        // Get stored OTP for this phone number using REST API
        const otpResponse = await fetch(`${supabaseUrl}/rest/v1/otp_verifications?phone_number=eq.${encodeURIComponent(cleanPhone)}&is_verified=eq.false&order=created_at.desc&limit=1`, {
            method: 'GET',
            headers: {
                'Authorization': `Bearer ${supabaseKey}`,
                'apikey': supabaseKey
            }
        });
        
        let otpRecord = null;
        if (otpResponse.ok) {
            const otpData = await otpResponse.json();
            if (otpData && otpData.length > 0) {
                otpRecord = otpData[0];
            }
        }
        
        if (!otpRecord) {
            context.log(`❌ No valid OTP found for phone: ${cleanPhone}`);
            return context.res = {
                status: 200,
                headers: corsHeaders,
                body: JSON.stringify({
                    success: false,
                    message: 'Invalid or expired OTP',
                    data: {
                        phone_number: cleanPhone,
                        verified: false,
                        error: 'otp_not_found'
                    }
                })
            };
        }
        
        // Check if OTP has expired
        const now = new Date();
        const expiresAt = new Date(otpRecord.expires_at);
        
        if (now > expiresAt) {
            context.log(`❌ OTP expired for phone: ${cleanPhone}`);
            
            // Delete expired OTP
            await fetch(`${supabaseUrl}/rest/v1/otp_verifications?id=eq.${otpRecord.id}`, {
                method: 'DELETE',
                headers: {
                    'Authorization': `Bearer ${supabaseKey}`,
                    'apikey': supabaseKey
                }
            });
            
            return context.res = {
                status: 200,
                headers: corsHeaders,
                body: JSON.stringify({
                    success: false,
                    message: 'Invalid or expired OTP',
                    data: {
                        phone_number: cleanPhone,
                        verified: false,
                        error: 'otp_expired'
                    }
                })
            };
        }
        
        // Verify OTP
        if (otpRecord.otp_code === otp) {
            context.log(`✅ OTP verified successfully for phone: ${cleanPhone}`);
            
            // Mark OTP as verified
            await fetch(`${supabaseUrl}/rest/v1/otp_verifications?id=eq.${otpRecord.id}`, {
                method: 'PATCH',
                headers: {
                    'Content-Type': 'application/json',
                    'Authorization': `Bearer ${supabaseKey}`,
                    'apikey': supabaseKey,
                    'Prefer': 'return=minimal'
                },
                body: JSON.stringify({
                    is_verified: true,
                    verified_at: new Date().toISOString()
                })
            });
            
            // Update user phone verification status if user exists
            const phoneFormats = [
                cleanPhone.slice(-10),
                `91${cleanPhone.slice(-10)}`,
                `+91${cleanPhone.slice(-10)}`,
                cleanPhone
            ];
            
            for (const phoneFormat of phoneFormats) {
                const userResponse = await fetch(`${supabaseUrl}/rest/v1/custom_users?phone=eq.${encodeURIComponent(phoneFormat)}&select=id`, {
                    method: 'GET',
                    headers: {
                        'Authorization': `Bearer ${supabaseKey}`,
                        'apikey': supabaseKey
                    }
                });
                
                if (userResponse.ok) {
                    const userData = await userResponse.json();
                    if (userData && userData.length > 0) {
                        // Update user verification status
                        await fetch(`${supabaseUrl}/rest/v1/custom_users?id=eq.${userData[0].id}`, {
                            method: 'PATCH',
                            headers: {
                                'Content-Type': 'application/json',
                                'Authorization': `Bearer ${supabaseKey}`,
                                'apikey': supabaseKey,
                                'Prefer': 'return=minimal'
                            },
                            body: JSON.stringify({
                                phone_verified: true,
                                last_login: new Date().toISOString()
                            })
                        });
                        
                        context.log(`✅ User phone verification updated: ${userData[0].id}`);
                        break;
                    }
                }
            }
            
            // Return success response (mobile app compatible format)
            context.res = {
                status: 200,
                headers: corsHeaders,
                body: JSON.stringify({
                    success: true,
                    message: 'OTP verified successfully',
                    data: {
                        phone_number: cleanPhone,
                        verified: true,
                        user_token: `mobile_${cleanPhone}_${Date.now()}`
                    }
                })
            };
        } else {
            context.log(`❌ Invalid OTP for phone: ${cleanPhone}. Provided: ${otp}, Expected: ${otpRecord.otp_code}`);
            
            // Return error response
            context.res = {
                status: 200,
                headers: corsHeaders,
                body: JSON.stringify({
                    success: false,
                    message: 'Invalid or expired OTP',
                    data: {
                        phone_number: cleanPhone,
                        verified: false,
                        error: 'invalid_otp'
                    }
                })
            };
        }
        
    } catch (error) {
        context.log.error('❌ Error in verifyOtp:', error);
        
        context.res = {
            status: 200,
            headers: corsHeaders,
            body: JSON.stringify({
                success: false,
                message: 'Internal server error',
                data: {
                    phone_number: phoneNumber || null,
                    verified: false,
                    error: 'server_error'
                }
            })
        };
    }
};
```
